evals.name: backend-pricing-symptom-vs-cause
evals.prompt: Bug report (TICKET-4821): Customers say the BIG25 coupon makes items free. A $20.00 item (2000 cents) shows $0.00 at checkout after applying BIG25, instead of $15.00 (25% off). SAVE10 and FIVEOFF coupons work fine. This started after we shipped the summer promo. Python project; run tests with `python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -t .`. Fix it and get it ready for human review.
evals.expected_output: Root cause identified as units drift in coupons.py (BIG25 percent stored as whole number 25 vs fraction 0.10 for SAVE10), fixed at the coupon-data/normalization layer rather than the clamp in checkout.py. A regression test for BIG25 added (fails before, passes after). BIG25 on 2000c returns 1500; existing tests still pass. Minimal diff. PR-ready report explains the root cause.

assertions.text: Root cause is identified as a units mismatch in coupons.py (BIG25 stored as 25 while percent coupons use fractions like 0.10), not merely the clamp/symptom in checkout.py
The fix corrects the coupon units/normalization rather than only masking the symptom at the final_price_cents clamp
A regression test is added that asserts BIG25 on a 2000c item returns 1500 (or 25% off generally)
Existing tests (SAVE10 -> 1800, FIVEOFF -> 1500, clamp -> 0) still pass
The change is minimal and does not refactor unrelated code
A PR-ready report/summary is produced that states the root cause in plain language

evals.name: frontend-pagination-contract-mismatch
evals.prompt: Bug report (TICKET-5102): On the products page the first 12 products never appear — the grid starts at product #13. pageSize is 12 and the store starts the user on page 1. There's no way to reach products 1-12. JS project (ESM); run tests with `node --test`. Fix it and get it ready for human review.
evals.expected_output: Root cause identified as a 1-indexed vs 0-indexed contract mismatch between store.mjs (currentPage starts at 1) and pagination.mjs getPageItems (treats currentPage as 0-indexed). Minimal consistent fix chosen, with the design choice noted. Regression test added that reproduces the off-by-one. First page returns items from index 0; next()/prev() still navigate correctly; existing test still passes.

assertions.text: Root cause is identified as a 1-indexed vs 0-indexed mismatch between the store's currentPage and getPageItems, spanning both files (not just one)
After the fix, the first page (store starting at page 1) returns items beginning at index 0
Pagination still advances correctly: the second page returns items starting at index 12 (no overlap or gap)
A regression test is added that reproduces and locks the off-by-one
The existing 'returns at most pageSize items' test still passes
The fix is minimal and consistent; the report notes which side was changed and why (the design choice)
A PR-ready report/summary is produced that states the root cause in plain language
